seo.title: Denis Kukobin — QA Lead | Senior QA Engineer | Frontend Developer
seo.description: QA Lead with 6+ years in iGaming: 21 microservices, Playwright automation, AI-assisted testing as a company standard. Team of 14. Open to opportunities.

description.label: Who I am as a dev

title.name: QA Engineer
title.headline: I find what will kill your production — before your users do.

description.body: Six years in QA on products that don't forgive mistakes: iGaming, blockchain, LLM agents. Leading 14 engineers at SprutGaming — 21 microservices, Go, Kafka, payment flows with real money. Came from frontend, so I read code and find root causes — not symptoms. First in the company to introduce AI-assisted QA as a formal standard. I build quality as a system property, not a pre-release checklist.

items: I read code, not just UI — came from React/Next.js frontend. I find the root cause of a bug in minutes, not hours.
Tested non-standard products with no existing playbooks: L2 ETH DEX, LLM chat with agents, crypto platforms — wrote the approaches from zero.
iGaming scale: 21 microservices, payment integrations (crypto invoice, H2H, fiat), Go services, Kafka, Kubernetes. Bugs in payments are real money.
First in the company to introduce AI-assisted QA: LLM DB migration verification, Cursor IDE + Claude + Figma MCP. A company standard since 2025.
Built QA from scratch twice: zero coverage to full cycle — checklists, test cases, AC review, smoke + e2e, DoD — in 6 months.

items.detail: First QA role in a company that had none before. White-box testing of a CRM, frontend in React alongside. Ended up as the sole QA on the project.
Several non-standard products back to back: an author social network, an L2 ETH DEX, a crypto platform. Playwright, Jest, ~70% coverage, mobile testing via TestFlight.
LLM chat with MCP servers and agent tool calls — a product with no existing testing playbooks. Wrote the approaches from scratch.
iGaming B2B platform: white-label casinos, payment flows (crypto invoice, H2H, fiat), 21 microservices. Introduced AI-assisted QA as a team workflow.
Team of 14 (9 core + 5 contractors). Built processes from zero, onboarded 8+ engineers, syncs with CTO 3× a week.

items: Become a public voice on AI-assisted QA — it's a hot topic with almost no quality content yet.
Shift-left as a cultural norm: quality is designed into requirements, not patched after release.
Grow engineers who think in systems and risks — not ones chasing task counts.
Cut escaped defects and MTTR through observability, automation, and ownership culture.
Build teams that run without my hands-on control — because good processes scale, managers don't.

items.title: AI-assisted QA — company regulation, not a pilot
items.sub: LLM DB migration verification, pixel-perfect via AI, e2e with Cursor IDE + Claude and Figma MCP. Rolled out in 2025 — before it became an industry trend.

items.title: QA department from zero to standard in 6 months
items.sub: Day one: no test cases, no documentation. Result: full cycle — checklists, AC review, smoke + e2e, DoD — adopted company-wide.

items.title: 14 engineers hired — zero attrition
items.sub: Onboarded 8+ engineers, all still with the company. Led 9 core QA + 5 contractors, daily prioritization, C-level reporting 3× a week.

items.title: LLM product testing methodology — written from scratch
items.sub: Streaming AI responses, MCP servers, agent tool calls, crypto subscriptions. No playbooks existed — built the approaches the team still uses today.

attachments.intro: What usually ships as files: recent QA writing and direct contacts.

title.headline: I ship products to production. Code first, everything else when needed.

description.body: Frontend developer who doesn't stop at 'UI is done'. 8+ products shipped to production: from social networks to a DEX on a custom L2 Ethereum. In 2024 I built an LLM chat with MCP agents — when agentic AI wasn't yet mainstream. React/Next.js is my core, Node.js backend when needed, can read and fix Go. I know what production looks like from the inside — because I've shipped there myself.

projects.name: Papiny Skazki
projects.desc: Bedtime story service for parents: the AI generates a new story every day from a short description. Next.js, Supabase, SCSS — solo from idea to deploy.

projects.name: LLM Chat + MCP Agents
projects.desc: LLM chat with external MCP servers from GitHub and custom agent creation — built in 2024, a year before agentic AI became the mainstream topic.

projects.name: DEX on L2 Ethereum
projects.desc: Token swap platform on a custom Layer 2 blockchain. Full cycle: React UI, smart contract integration via Ethers.js, production deployment.

projects.name: Sprint Board
projects.desc: Internal dashboard with Jira API: sprint progress, team workload, real-time metrics. Built over a weekend — the whole company still uses it.

items.message: feat: first commercial React app @ VegaAvangard
items.detail: CRM system in React for a company that had no frontend before. First production, first real deadline — both shipped.
DEX on custom L2 Ethereum, crypto investor social network, writers media platform, LLM chat with MCP agents. Each one: full cycle from design to production.

items.message: feat: internal tooling + AI integrations @ SprutGaming
items.detail: Sprint Board for the entire company, AI pipelines, internal tools. Projects that are actually used — not gathering dust in Confluence.
This portfolio on Next.js 16 + Framer Motion. Papiny Skazki. Going public — because it's time to build a personal brand.

items.name: LLM agents + MCP — before the mainstream hype (2024)
DEX on custom L2 Ethereum (React + Ethers.js)
